steps.title: Recognize the grammatical structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: [singular subject “The collection”] + [blank] + [direct object]. This is an independent clause that requires a finite main verb—not an infinitive, gerund, or participle form.

steps.title: Apply the verb agreement rule
steps.content: “Includes” is the third-person singular present tense verb that correctly agrees with the singular subject “The collection.” Options A and C offer infinitives, and option B offers a gerund/participle—none of which can serve as main verbs. That matches choice D — D is correct.
